raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The intersection of the criminal legal system and housing insecurity is costly in many ways.",
    "In Los Angeles, approximately $70 million is spent annually on incarceration costs for individuals trapped in the homelessness-jail cycle.",
    "Over 30,000 people will be released on parole or early release each year over the next five years.",
    "The majority of formerly incarcerated people experienced housing insecurity or homelessness prior to their incarceration.",
    "AB-1816 will help prevent individuals just released from incarceration from falling into a cycle of homelessness.",
    "Supportive housing and employment are more cost-effective than incarceration.",
    "Investing in reentry housing and workforce development will result in significant savings for the state.",
    "70% of people experiencing homelessness in California have a history of incarceration.",
    "Data show that people on parole are about 10 times more likely to experience homelessness than the general public.",
    "Providing formerly incarcerated individuals experiencing homelessness with stable, affordable housing will reduce recidivism."
  ]
}
```
